selected_messages.role_discriminator: Vote.Vote.Type is types.PrevoteType or types.PrecommitType; nil refers to the signed vote BlockID, not a separate native message type

outbound_entries: Consensus Reactor VoteSetMaj23 receive reply -> Envelope.Src.TrySend

excluded_messages.reason: exclusively transaction propagation; route is distinct from all selected routes
exclusively Evidence Reactor traffic; route is distinct from all selected routes
exclusively block synchronization; route is distinct from all selected routes
exclusively peer exchange; route is distinct from all selected routes

excluded_messages.owner: outside the registered P2P Reactor Channel set
excluded_messages.reason: the production Switch registrations contain only MEMPOOL, BLOCKSYNC, CONSENSUS, EVIDENCE, STATESYNC, and optional PEX; RPC/application paths are not P2P Channels
State Sync registers only Snapshot and Chunk Channels, and its wrapper registry contains only SnapshotsRequest, SnapshotsResponse, ChunkRequest, and ChunkResponse

covered_send_modes.existing_result: bool; false if peer stopped, route unsupported, marshal fails, or native queue cannot accept within 10 seconds
bool; immediately false if stopped, unsupported, marshal fails, or queue is full

covered_send_modes.convergence: one goroutine per current peer -> Peer.Send
one goroutine per current peer -> Peer.TrySend

entries: State Sync repeated snapshot discovery hook

selected_boundary.reason: narrowest shared existing boundary retaining authenticated destination Peer.ID, route, concrete native protobuf message, and the blocking/non-blocking send continuation
selected_boundary.disabled_mode_obligation: the existing wrap, marshal, MConnection call, bool result, and metrics path must remain byte-for-byte and behaviorally unchanged when disabled

bypass_risks.risk: MConnection.Send and TrySend are lower byte-level APIs
bypass_risks.finding: production p2p source calls them only from (*peer).Send/TrySend via (*peer).send; they are too low for native classification
bypass_risks.planning_constraint: do not expose a new selected caller path directly to MConnection; focused tests must prove no direct selected send

bypass_risks.risk: new or custom Peer implementations can implement the public Peer interface without (*peer).send
bypass_risks.finding: ordinary MultiplexTransport creates *peer for both accepted and dialed connections; custom test/mocked Peers bypass the concrete boundary
bypass_risks.planning_constraint: production assembly and tests must make interception ownership explicit and test custom/mocked boundary behavior where used

bypass_risks.risk: broadcast APIs discard per-peer results and spawn goroutines
bypass_risks.finding: an asynchronous failure can occur after the broadcast caller returned
bypass_risks.planning_constraint: accepted-work loss or controller failure must reach fatal ownership rather than silently disappear

bypass_risks.risk: selected direct P2P inbound traffic currently reaches Reactor.Receive
bypass_risks.finding: selected routes are exclusive in this revision and can be recognized before native dispatch
bypass_risks.planning_constraint: instrumentation mode must reject these routes before Reactor.Receive while disabled mode preserves the existing chain

ordinary_receive_chain.existing_fact: accepted and dialed sockets are upgraded to SecretConnection; remote signatures authenticate RemotePubKey, and the derived ID must match dialed and self-reported NodeInfo IDs
MultiplexTransport wraps both connection directions as *peer with the same route, prototype, and Reactor maps
MConnection.recvRoutine validates the byte Channel, enforces Channel receive capacity while assembling packets, and invokes onReceive(channelID, msgBytes) synchronously in its receive goroutine

createMConnection.onReceive looks up the owning Reactor and wrapper prototype by Channel, clones the prototype, decodes native bytes with gogoproto, unwraps via types.Unwrapper, records receive-byte metrics, attributes Src to the authenticated *peer, and calls Reactor.Receive(Envelope)
panic from unknown route, decode, unwrap, or Reactor execution is recovered by MConnection and reported via onPeerError to Switch.StopPeerForError

Consensus Receive converts protobuf to internal message, validates it, stops invalid peers, obtains PeerState, then updates peer state or enqueues Proposal/BlockPart/Vote work; State Sync Receive validates protobuf, stops invalid peers, and dispatches by Channel and type

route_registry.existing_fact: Switch.AddReactor uniquely binds Channel ID to ChannelDescriptor.MessageType and owning Reactor; duplicate route ownership panics

candidate_reinjection_entry.registry_reuse: Switch Channel -> prototype and Reactor bindings plus PeerSet.Get(p2p.ID)
candidate_reinjection_entry.reason: this is the same existing entry reached after ordinary P2P decode/unwrap/source attribution and preserves native Reactor validation and peer-error behavior
candidate_reinjection_entry.credible_implementation_alternative: factor the existing decode/unwrap/dispatch body behind a p2p-owned helper used by both MConnection and callback delivery; Prompt 02 must choose concrete structure

validation_before_acceptance: HTTP method/path/content type and bounded body
complete outer JSON fields with exact capitalization
To equals local NodeKey-derived stable P2P identity
From parses as p2p.ID and resolves to a currently running authenticated Peer
adapter payload version/length and route identifier are valid
route is registered, selected, and has a local prototype and owning Reactor
native wrapped bytes are within that Channel's RecvMessageCapacity
ordinary prototype clone, protobuf decode, and unwrap succeed
decoded concrete type belongs to the selected route
revision-local pure basic semantic validation succeeds (Consensus MsgFromProto including ValidateBasic, or State Sync validateMsg) before acceptance; stateful Reactor handling remains after handoff
recomputed deterministic label matches outer Type
bounded reinjection queue accepts responsibility before returning 2xx
queued_stable_data: From and To stable identity strings
immutable copy of exact ordinary wrapped protobuf bytes
dispatch_time_lookups: re-resolve From through current Switch.PeerSet by authenticated p2p.ID and require the peer to remain current/running
re-resolve Channel prototype and owning Reactor or verify a lifecycle-frozen registry
decode and unwrap from queued exact bytes to avoid sharing a mutable proto.Message
construct p2p.Envelope with current Peer, original Channel ID, and concrete message
callback_contract.acceptance_boundary: 2xx means all validation succeeded and immutable work was accepted by a bounded queue for native handoff; it does not mean Reactor.Receive returned, protocol validation succeeded, application work ran, state changed, or data persisted
callback_contract.post_acceptance_responsibility: shutdown races, stale-peer discovery, registry/invariant failure, queue corruption, worker failure, or any other accepted-work loss must be visible as reinjection failure and fatal instrumentation integrity failure, never a silent drop
callback_contract.ordering_and_multiplicity: one queued item per accepted callback, FIFO by callback acceptance; preserve duplicate ID and never deduplicate or restore source-send order
hazards: Consensus Receive may block while enqueueing Proposal, BlockPart, or Vote to peerMsgQueue
native validation may synchronously call Switch.StopPeerForError and remove the source Peer
State Sync Receive can hold Reactor locks and issue blocking reply sends
shutdown can race accepted queued work
Switch Reactor maps are documented as not goroutine-safe

local_identity.existing_fact: NodeKey is persisted in config/node_key.json; NodeKey.ID is the hex public-key address and is installed in NodeInfo and Switch

remote_identity.existing_fact: Peer.ID returns authenticated NodeInfo.ID; SecretConnection RemotePubKey-derived ID must match NodeInfo and, for outbound connections, the dialed ID
remote_identity.use: outbound To and callback From lookup

peer_lookup_and_replacement.existing_fact: Switch.PeerSet is a mutex-protected ID -> current Peer map with Get; duplicate live IDs are rejected
peer_lookup_and_replacement.accepted_peers: acceptRoutine authenticates, wraps, then calls addPeer
peer_lookup_and_replacement.dialed_peers: DialPeerWithAddress authenticates, wraps, then calls the same addPeer
peer_lookup_and_replacement.disconnect_removal: stopAndRemovePeer stops the peer, cleans transport and Reactor state, then removes it from PeerSet last
peer_lookup_and_replacement.reconnection: persistent reconnect creates and adds a new Peer for the same stable ID after removal
peer_lookup_and_replacement.planning_constraint: never retain a Peer pointer across asynchronous callback handoff; re-resolve the ID

configuration.existing_fact: Config defaults, Viper/TOML loading, Config.ValidateBasic, and config.toml.tpl are the ordinary configuration path; existing Config.Instrumentation is specifically Prometheus metrics
configuration.required_attachment: default-off controller mediation with controller address, callback listen/advertise address, and integer experiment node ID, validated before node construction/start

construction.existing_fact: DefaultNewNode loads persistent NodeKey and calls NewNodeWithCliParams; that function creates native Reactors, MultiplexTransport, Switch registrations, and the Node
construction.required_attachment: reuse this construction path and existing transport/peer lifecycle

readiness_and_startup.existing_fact: Node.OnStart currently starts optional servers, binds P2P transport, starts Switch/Reactors, dials persistent peers, optionally starts State Sync, then starts pruning
readiness_and_startup.planning_constraint: callback bind, worker readiness, and successful controller registration must precede Switch activity that can create selected sends; readiness cannot depend on State Sync or SwitchToConsensus

shutdown.existing_fact: Node.OnStop stops services and Switch, closes transport and listeners, and then stores; Switch.OnStop removes peers and stops Reactors
shutdown.planning_constraint: controller requests/workers/listener must be canceled and accepted work accounted for during this owner-driven shutdown, including partial startup

testnet_generation.existing_fact: commands.testnetFiles uses one Config to initialize per-node roots/keys and then rewrites each config by node index; hostnameOrIP already derives per-node hosts
testnet_generation.required_attachment: when mediation is enabled for generated nodes, assign unique integer IDs and callback addresses plus common controller address without duplicating the generator

fatal_propagation.existing_fact: startup creation/Start errors return through Cobra RunE and main exits non-zero by panicking on Execute error; after startup RunNode blocks forever and BaseService.Quit carries no error cause while OnStop cannot return an error
fatal_propagation.planning_constraint: runtime instrumentation fatal cause needs a typed, orderly path to Node owner/command runner so cleanup completes and RunE returns an error; deep code must not panic or os.Exit

implementation_constraints.constraint: Disabled mode must preserve (*peer).send's stopped-peer/channel checks, wrapper selection, exact gogoproto bytes, metrics, bool results, native 10-second Send bound, and immediate TrySend behavior.

Selected classification must cover exactly the ten Consensus and four State Sync concrete types on six exclusive routes; an unlabelled valid selected type or registered selected route without decoder/handler is fatal.
Controller adapter bytes must contain route plus the exact bytes produced after native Wrapper.Wrap and gogoproto marshal; callback must use the registered wrapper prototype, decode, and Unwrap.
Request and decoded payload limits must respect 1 MiB Consensus, 4,000,000-byte Snapshot, and 16,000,000-byte Chunk RecvMessageCapacity values, plus bounded adapter framing/base64 overhead.

Peer.Send is allowed to wait no longer than the native 10-second queue timeout; Peer.TrySend and TryBroadcast-originated work must not gain blocking lookup, lock, serialization, HTTP, or queue behavior.
Broadcast has no result channel and per-peer work is already asynchronous; any success reported before controller submission completes creates fatal post-acceptance ownership on later failure.
PeerSet.ForEach holds its mutex while invoking the callback, so instrumentation must not perform blocking controller or queue work while that peer-set lock is held.
Native Reactor.Receive is not a harmless callback: it may block, mutate PeerState, enqueue consensus work, send replies, or stop/remove a peer. No HTTP/queue/peer-map lock may be held across it.
Switch AddReactor/RemoveReactor maps are not goroutine-safe and custom Reactors can replace standard registrations during construction; registry lifetime must be explicitly owned.
Do not reuse Config.Instrumentation ambiguously: in this revision that section and type are Prometheus metrics configuration.
The standard library net/http and encoding/json are sufficient and already used by Node servers; no production web-framework dependency is needed.

candidate_surface.likely_role: shared outbound interception and ordinary codec/native-dispatch reuse
route registry, current authenticated peer lookup, lifecycle, direct-inbound gate
instrumentation diagnostics/metrics attachment without Consensus state mutation
default-off configuration and early validation while preserving metrics config
assembly, readiness order, shutdown ownership, runtime fatal propagation
ordinary constructor and Reactor/Switch registration attachment
owning command's orderly non-zero runtime fatal result
unique generated callback address and experiment ID

test_anchors.use: native blocking/TrySend convergence, wrapper bytes, bool behavior
broadcast, both connection directions, peer removal/replacement, source lookup lifecycle
all Consensus concrete types, wrapper/codec vectors
native receive validation and selected role coverage
all State Sync concrete types, bounds and wrapper/codec vectors
native State Sync receive, replies, source peer

assumptions: The ordinary production node uses MultiplexTransport and the concrete *p2p.peer created by node.NewNodeWithCliParams; custom application-supplied transports or Peer implementations require separate explicit integration if placed in scope later.

Reactor registrations are construction-time state in the ordinary node; because mutation APIs are not goroutine-safe, a later implementation must explicitly freeze or own callback lookup rather than assume runtime mutation safety.
Simple deterministic labels listed here are revision-local controller metadata; native route and exact wrapped bytes remain authoritative and the planner must test the complete mapping.
A callback queue may be the specification-defined native handoff boundary only if it is bounded, preserves FIFO acceptance order/multiplicity, and makes any accepted-work loss fatal.
unresolved_risks: The concrete runtime fatal-error propagation structure is absent today: RunNode blocks forever after startup and BaseService.Quit has no cause. Prompt 02 must bind an orderly typed owner path without changing disabled behavior.
A safe route-registry access API or frozen snapshot does not currently exist; Prompt 02 must resolve ownership without concurrent access to non-thread-safe Switch registration maps.
The exact adapter payload framing/version and HTTP body limit remain planning decisions, constrained to route plus exact wrapped bytes and the discovered native capacities.
Broadcast APIs discard per-peer results, so asynchronous acceptance and fatal ownership require careful later synchronization design.
Native Reactor.Receive may synchronously stop the source peer or block; callback acceptance must remain a bounded handoff and later dispatch must account for peer disappearance and shutdown.
Controller HTTP timeout/retry values are not present in current source and must be chosen within the specification and existing 10-second blocking-send bound.
Existing Config.Instrumentation naming collides semantically with the experiment's generic term instrumentation; later naming must avoid breaking Prometheus configuration.
